title: ECB Annual Accounts 2025
summary: The ECB published its 2025 Annual Accounts on February 26, 2026, reporting a balance sheet of €603.3 billion, contracted by €37.3 billion from 2024, primarily due to ceased reinvestment of maturing securities under APP programs. The ECB recorded a loss of €1,254 million, significantly lower than 2024, driven by reduced interest expense on net TARGET liabilities.
key_points: ECB balance sheet contracted €37.3 billion in 2025 to €603.3 billion, mainly due to no reinvestment of principal from maturing APP securities
ECB reported a loss of €1,254 million for 2025, substantially lower than 2024 losses
Loss reduction attributed to decreased interest expense from net TARGET liability
Losses will remain on balance sheet to be offset against future profits
Annual Accounts published each February as part of ECB Annual Report
why_it_matters: The ECB's financial position and loss trajectory provide insight into the central bank's monetary policy implementation costs and potential constraints on future operations. The €37.3 billion balance sheet contraction reflects passive QT through ceased APP reinvestments.
macro_context: Backward-looking accounting data; actual ECB balance sheet dynamics more relevant for market participants than the annual accounting summary. Shows ongoing passive QT through APP portfolio wind-down.
